message:
chore(beads): close bd-hudcn.1.7.1, bd-hudcn.1.8.1, bd-hudcn.1.9.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -450,7 +450,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2k018.1","title":"Audit table styling touchpoints + constraints","description":"Purpose:
- Enumerate every table rendering touchpoint and its styling inputs so the new TableTheme can replace ad-hoc styling across the codebase.

Scope:
- ftui-widgets Table rendering path (header/row/zebra/selection handling).
- ftui-extras markdown table rendering (MarkdownTheme table fields, width fitting, borders).
- Demo showcase screens using Table widgets or markdown tables (e.g., dashboard, terminal_capabilities, widget_gallery).

Deliverables:
- File/function map of all table styling touchpoints.
- Notes on current Style/ColorToken usage and how to thread a shared TableTheme.
- Test/snapshot surfaces affected (unit + snapshot + E2E).
- Determinism constraints (where time/phase must be explicit).

Acceptance:
- A concise, self-contained map of all touchpoints and constraints is captured in the bead description/notes.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:23.896250402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:42.400099867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:42.400082645Z","close_reason":"Touchpoint map added in comments","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:23.896250402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":629,"issue_id":"bd-2k018.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Touchpoints: ftui-widgets Table
- File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/table.rs
- Styles on Table: base style via Table::style (applied to entire table_area with set_style_area), highlight_style via Table::highlight_style (merged with row.style for selected row), column_spacing affects layout
- Styles on Row: Row::style (applied per row; header Row::style used for header area)
- Borders: Table optionally wraps Block (Block::render + Block::inner) for borders/titles; Block styling is independent of table styles
- Rendering flow: Table::render -> apply base style to table_area, render header + rows via render_row; render_row merges span style with row/table style, uses draw_text_span_with_link
- Constraints: uses Frame.degradation.apply_styling() to skip styles at lower degradation; pushes scissor to clip rows; column widths computed via Flex::horizontal + intrinsic widths (Text::width)

Touchpoints: ftui-extras Markdown tables
- File: crates/ftui-extras/src/markdown.rs
- Theme fields: MarkdownTheme::table_border/table_header/table_row/table_row_alt
- Rendering: RenderState::flush_table builds widths, applies table_max_width, emits border lines via table_border_line; row lines via table_row_line with base_style + border_style; cell spans styled in apply_table_cell_style
- Constraints: table_max_width (MarkdownRenderer::table_max_width) caps content width; table_cell_spans truncates with ellipsis using Span::split_at_cell (width-sensitive); table layout uses Unicode line-drawing characters

Demo showcase usage / overrides
- Table widget usage:
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/widget_gallery.rs (Table::new + header Row::style + Table::style + Block border theme)
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/intrinsic_sizing.rs (Table::new + header Row::style + Table::style + Block border theme)
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/terminal_capabilities.rs (Table::new + header Row::style; per-row Row::style for highlight/mismatch; Table::style + Block border theme)
- Markdown table styling overrides:
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/markdown_rich_text.rs (MarkdownTheme overrides for table_* styles; MarkdownRenderer::table_max_width)
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/markdown_live_editor.rs (MarkdownTheme overrides for table_* styles; table_max_width)
  - crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/dashboard.rs (MarkdownRenderer::table_max_width; wrap_markdown_for_panel avoids wrapping table-like lines)

Constraints/notes
- Determinism: table widths derived from Text/Span width metrics; no randomization
- Performance: table_row_line builds spans per cell; markdown table truncation uses per-span width and split_at_cell; table widget uses set_style_area to clear gaps
","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k018.10","title":"Widget Table render tests + snapshots","description":"Purpose:
- Prove TableTheme integration in ftui-widgets Table renders correctly.

Test cases:
- Header/border/zebra styles applied from TableTheme.
- Selected/hover row styling correct (if supported).
- Animated highlight row with fixed phase matches snapshot.
- Column spacing + padding + row_height produce expected layout.
- Wide-glyph rows (emoji/CJK) render with correct column widths.

Logging:
- Snapshot metadata includes preset name + fixed phase; log resolved style hash on failure.

Acceptance:
- Widget table snapshots match expected visuals and are deterministic.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:37.755407493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:08:41.317460582Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:08:41.317441967Z","close_reason":"Added widget table snapshot + ANSI tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:37.755407493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:02:35.664380391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:45:06.109793388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:45:02.728832879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k018.11","title":"Markdown table render tests + snapshots","description":"Purpose:
- Prove markdown table rendering uses TableTheme correctly.

Test cases:
- Borders/header/zebra styles match TableTheme preset.
- Table width fitting preserved.
- Animated highlight row with fixed phase matches snapshot.
- Alignment (left/center/right), escaped pipes, multi-line cells, wide graphemes.

Logging:
- Snapshot metadata includes preset name + fixed phase; log resolved style hash on failure.

Acceptance:
- Markdown table snapshots are deterministic and match widget table styling.

-{"id":"bd-2k018.12","title":"E2E: table theming validation + detailed logging","description":"Purpose:
- Provide end-to-end validation that table theming and animation render correctly in the demo app.

Scope:
- Extend existing E2E scripts to cover themed table screens.
- Emit structured JSONL logs with fields: preset_id, phase, highlight_target, resolved_style_hash, snapshot/checksum, screen_id, table_dims, column_widths, terminal_size.
- Validate widget + markdown tables against the same theme where possible and log parity results.
- Include export/import actions (custom presets) in E2E runs when available; log export target (file/clipboard) and checksum.
- Ensure phase is fixed (deterministic) for E2E runs.

Acceptance:
- E2E run produces clear JSONL evidence for table theming + layout correctness.
- Failures are actionable with preset/phase/target + checksum or screenshot path.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.13","title":"API ergonomics + re-exports for TableTheme","description":"Purpose:
- Make TableTheme trivial to use and discover by end users.

Scope:
- Provide builder helpers (with_* methods) and preset constructors.
- Re-export TableTheme and presets from ftui prelude or theme module.
- Ensure documentation examples compile with minimal imports.

Acceptance:
- Users can adopt TableTheme with a few lines (no deep module spelunking).
- Presets are discoverable and documented.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.14","title":"Preset contrast + a11y validation","description":"Purpose:
- Ensure presets remain readable under dense layouts and a11y modes.

Scope:
- Evaluate preset contrast ratios for header/row/zebra/border combinations.
- Provide adjustments or fallback styling for high-contrast / large-text modes.
- Add targeted tests for contrast thresholds where feasible.

Acceptance:
- Presets remain legible across themes and a11y modes.
- Document any constraints or fallback behavior.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:11.869331206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T23:57:01.152818965Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T23:57:01.152800600Z","close_reason":"Added WCAG contrast validation test for table presets; adjusted divider colors (Graphite/Slate/Midnight) to meet AA-large; documented ANSI16 hover constraint and tightened TerminalClassic hover bg for non-ANSI.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:11.869331206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:53:48.409522047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:19.775574255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k018.15","title":"Implement TableTheme core + presets","description":"Purpose:
- Implement the shared TableTheme struct and built-in presets (aurora/graphite/neon + expanded presets) that other tasks will consume.

Scope:
- Define TableTheme fields per spec (border/header/row/alt/selected/hover/divider/padding/column_gap/row_height/effects).
- Implement preset constructors with strong readability defaults.
- Ensure presets respect ColorToken-based themes and dynamic theme switching.
- Provide default style_mask behavior for effects (e.g., fg/bg only unless specified).
- Add new presets per spec: slate, solar, orchard, paper, midnight, terminal_classic.

terminal_classic constraints:
- Use ANSI 16‑color baseline but allow richer palettes on 256/truecolor terminals so it doesn’t look worse on modern terminals.

Constraints:
- No hot-path allocations in resolve/render.
- Presets must be legible in 80x24 and 120x40 layouts.

Acceptance:
- TableTheme core exists and presets render sensibly with no animation enabled.
- Presets are ready to be used by widget and markdown integrations.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:14.280186369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:34:28.430795777Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:34:28.430738359Z","close_reason":"Implemented TableTheme core + presets in ftui-style","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:14.280186369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:19.149619243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1154,15 +1154,15 @@
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","title":"Mermaid demo parity: eliminate unsupported placeholders for canonical supported families","description":"Background
- The Mermaid showcase is where users discover support quality. Unsupported badges for canonical families are unacceptable for release quality.

Goal
- Ensure the demo picker and gallery only surface canonical families with fully implemented rendering paths, and show no unsupported placeholders for those families.

Scope
- Update type picker behavior to reflect canonical registry support states.
- Remove or replace unsupported placeholders for families marked supported.
- Add family-specific interaction polish where needed (legend, metrics panel, fallback explanation, keyboard/mouse discoverability).

Deliverables
- Demo behavior where each canonical supported family renders meaningfully by default.
- Deterministic showcase paths for each family in scripted demo runs.

Acceptance Criteria
- No unsupported badge appears for families designated supported in bd-hudcn.1.1.
- Demo snapshots and PTY E2E confirm parity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:53.776011353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35.428363768Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","mermaid","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2d9fm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:40.369228894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2kn9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:40.065362215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-309ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:39.763363061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-30t8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:40.657952448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-9ta1z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:40.955054826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:53.776011353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:42.144906667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:42.443898091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.12.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:42.748025169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:43.045244145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:43.342615947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.15.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:43.641775836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.16.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:43.936379347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:44.235349200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:41.257779017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:41.550907567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:41.850882852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":913,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: this task is intentionally blocked by both legacy in-progress families (sequence/class/gantt/mindmap) and newly-added families so demo parity cannot be declared early. This ensures the showcase reflects true coverage state instead of a partial marketing surface.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","title":"CI completion gate: zero unsupported canonical Mermaid families","description":"Background
- We need a hard gate to prevent regression back to unsupported states after implementation lands.

Goal
- Add CI-level assertions that enforce zero unsupported canonical families and complete test coverage wiring for all families in the program.

Scope
- Add/extend automated checks that fail when:
  1) a canonical supported family resolves to unsupported in renderer/demo,
  2) a canonical family is missing from fixtures/snapshots/e2e manifests,
  3) warning/fallback codes diverge from policy.
- Integrate with existing snapshot and PTY validation jobs.

Deliverables
- Reproducible gate command(s) and CI wiring.
- Clear failure output keyed by family ID and fixture.

Acceptance Criteria
- A missing family implementation or regression fails CI deterministically.
- Gate references canonical registry from bd-hudcn.1.1 as single source of truth.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:59.922204215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35.718411761Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","mermaid","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yzfb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:46.249752798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2c2jj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.956941021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:59.922204215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:44.779144406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.076582091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.368183051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.657291926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":914,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: CI gate is deliberately downstream of parity + policy + fixtures + existing snapshot/e2e tasks. The intent is to make gate failures actionable by family and fixture, not generic 'coverage incomplete' noise.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","title":"Diagram Type: gitGraph (full terminal-native support)","description":"Background
- gitGraph is currently surfaced as unsupported in the demo and is a high-signal diagram family for developers.

Goal
- Implement full terminal-native gitGraph support (parser->IR->layout->render) with deterministic commit lane behavior and merge semantics.

Scope
- Support core gitGraph statements: commit/branch/checkout/merge/cherry-pick and styling/state annotations supported in our baseline.
- Produce clear lane assignment, branch divergence/convergence rendering, and readable commit labels under constrained widths.
- Ensure deterministic lane reuse and tie-break rules.

Acceptance Criteria
- gitGraph renders as first-class diagram in demo and markdown fences.
- Snapshot + PTY E2E evidence shows stable topology and labels.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:06.331136662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02.673597758Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","gitgraph","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:06.331136662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:24.541134022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:24.850729406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":902,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"gitGraph design intent:\n- prioritize topological correctness over decorative fidelity.\n- lane assignment must be deterministic with explicit tie-break rules (no heuristic nondeterminism).\n- merge visualization should remain understandable in narrow terminals; ASCII fallback must preserve branch ancestry semantics.\n- commit labels should degrade gracefully (truncate/ellipsis) while keeping branch/head references visible where possible.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.1","title":"gitGraph parser + IR normalization","description":"Goal
- Add robust gitGraph parsing and IR normalization with deterministic semantics.

Scope
- Extend tokenizer/parser for gitGraph directives and statement variants.
- Normalize into explicit commit DAG + lane events + branch-head pointers.
- Define deterministic tie-breaks for ambiguous branch/lane placement.
- Emit actionable diagnostics for malformed sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover representative branch/merge/cherry-pick flows and malformed input diagnostics.
- IR is stable and hashable for evidence logging.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldBear","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:12.825459379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:07:03.843434549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gitgraph","ir","mermaid","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:12.825459379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.2","title":"gitGraph layout + renderer (lanes, merges, labels, ASCII fallback)","description":"Goal

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","title":"Diagram Type: journey (full terminal-native support)","description":"Background
- journey diagrams are currently unsupported in demo output and are important for UX/story mapping use cases.

Goal
- Implement full journey diagram support with stage/section semantics and score visualization optimized for terminals.

Scope
- Parse journeys (sections, tasks, actors, scores/status markers).
- Render clear row/column structure with progress/intensity cues and compact legends.
- Support deterministic wrapping/truncation for long step labels.

Acceptance Criteria
- journey family renders as first-class in demo and markdown fences.
- Terminal output remains readable at 80x24 and scales gracefully.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:30.561417438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02.721769199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","journey","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:30.561417438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:25.150481022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:25.452834941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":904,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"journey design intent:\n- terminal output should make stage transitions and actor responsibilities immediately scannable.\n- score/status encoding must work in both color and low-capability/ascii fallback modes.\n- long task text is expected; wrapping/truncation policy should preserve the strongest semantic tokens first (stage/task identifiers).","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.9","title":"Diagram Type: requirementDiagram (full terminal-native support)","description":"Background

- Preserve identifiers, labels, and status fields under width constraints.
- Ensure deterministic placement and relationship routing.

- requirementDiagram renders in demo and markdown fences without unsupported fallbacks.
- Outputs remain legible and deterministic at target terminal sizes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:47.382708056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02.645454478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","mermaid","requirements"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:47.382708056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:25.761475597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:26.067631271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":901,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"requirementDiagram design intent:\n- preserve requirement identity and relationship semantics even when visual density is high.\n- prioritize stable entity ordering + relationship readability over aggressive compaction.\n- diagnostics should call out which requirement/link failed to parse so spec authors can fix source quickly.\n- fallback behavior must avoid silent drops of requirement entities/edges.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ic6i","title":"Internationalization (i18n) Foundation","description":"## Overview
Provide infrastructure for multi-language and RTL (right-to-left) text support in FrankenTUI applications.

## Rationale
- Global user base needs localized interfaces
- RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew) require proper text rendering and layout mirroring
- String externalization enables translation workflows
- Locale-aware formatting (dates, numbers) improves UX

## Core Components
1. **String Catalog System**: Externalized string storage with key-based lookup
2. **RTL Layout Mirroring**: Automatic flex/grid direction reversal for RTL locales
3. **Unicode BiDi Algorithm**: Proper bidirectional text rendering in cells
4. **Locale Context**: Thread-local or model-level locale state
5. **Pluralization Rules**: ICU-style plural forms for different languages

## Implementation Strategy
- Create ftui-i18n crate (optional feature-gated dependency)
- Integrate with ftui-text for BiDi text segment handling
- Modify ftui-layout to respect text direction context
- Provide macro for compile-time string extraction

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for BiDi text rendering (Arabic, Hebrew mixed with English)
- Layout tests for RTL flex/grid mirroring
- Snapshot tests comparing LTR vs RTL layouts
- Integration tests with demo showcase in multiple locales

## Success Criteria
- Applications can switch locale at runtime without restart
- RTL layouts render correctly with proper alignment
- No performance regression for LTR-only applications

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
